codeReview.summary: The feature substantially wires relay telemetry end-to-end (BrowserRelayClient EVENT/EOSE instrumentation, RuntimeRelayPump latency/event counters, slow-threshold constants, and dashboard rendering). However, Offline last-seen precedence is implemented incorrectly in both runtime relay-health renderers: when a relay has both `lastEventAt` and `lastDisconnectedAt`, the UI prefers `lastEventAt` instead of the disconnect timestamp. This conflicts with the feature’s stated VAL-SETTINGS-014 behavior and the in-code documentation that offline rows should prefer disconnect time.

issues.description: Offline rows compute `lastSeenSource` as `relay.lastEventAt ?? (...)`, so a stale pre-disconnect event timestamp overrides a newer `lastDisconnectedAt`. For VAL-SETTINGS-014 ('On disconnect ... Last Seen still real'), this can display an older, incorrect relative last-seen after disconnect instead of disconnect-time-relative copy.

The same precedence bug exists in `relayHealthRowsFromRuntime` (used by relays-offline rendering): `lastEventAt` is chosen before `lastDisconnectedAt`, despite the nearby comment stating offline relays should prefer disconnect time. This causes inconsistent/incorrect offline last-seen semantics when events were received before disconnect.
Offline last-seen test coverage only exercises `lastDisconnectedAt` without a concurrent `lastEventAt`, so it does not catch the precedence regression above.

sharedStateObservations.observation: runtime-worker procedure adherence is unclear for this UI-facing feature: required agent-browser/manual UI verification was skipped, and required verification commands from the skill were not fully run.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/runtime-worker/SKILL.md lines 22 and 49 require agent-browser/manual interactiveChecks for UI-surfaced behavior; line 44 and 46 require tsconfig.node + demo-gallery Playwright verification. Transcript skeleton for session 43ca2789-c544-4c01-a6b6-d04194e70ef7 shows only mission-worker-base/runtime-worker skills and no agent-browser invocation; handoff verification.commandsRun includes only tsc/vitest/git log (handoff lines 14,16,21,26).

sharedStateObservations.observation: The runtime-worker skill says omissions should be recorded via `followedProcedure: false` + deviations, but this handoff has no `skillFeedback` object, making procedure compliance non-auditable.

summary: Reviewed transcript skeleton, handoff, skill contract, and commit 8870273 diff. Result: fail due to a blocking offline last-seen precedence defect in both relay-health render paths (`lastEventAt` incorrectly overrides `lastDisconnectedAt`), plus a noted test gap that missed this case.
